scoping_test_cases.description: Happy path — full info provided, 3 core features, comfortable dev, 20h budget

messages.content: I want to build a crypto portfolio tracker. It shows a user's wallet balances across multiple chains and calculates total portfolio value in USD.
Nice — a multi-chain portfolio tracker. To plan this well, how much time do you have and how comfortable are you with the stack involved?
I have about 20 hours over 2 weeks. I'm comfortable with React and have done some Web3 work before.
Got it — comfortable developer, 20 hours. For core features I'm thinking: wallet connection, on-chain balance fetching, and a portfolio dashboard. Does that match what you have in mind?
Yes exactly. I also want price alerts but that can be later.
Perfect — price alerts go to nice-to-have. What does done look like for v1?
User connects wallet, sees all balances and total USD value on a dashboard.
Any constraints? Hard limits on tech or budget?
No paid APIs — free tier only. Stack is React, ethers.js, and a public RPC.
Any external dependencies beyond public RPC?
CoinGecko for prices.
What should we call this project?
ChainView

Project: ChainView
Vision: A multi-chain crypto portfolio tracker that shows wallet balances and USD value.
Target User: Crypto-active developers tracking their own holdings.
Problem: No simple self-hosted tool to view multi-chain balances in one place.
Available Time: ~20 hours over 2 weeks
Experience Level: Comfortable
Success Looks Like: User connects wallet and sees all balances and total USD value on a dashboard.
Constraints: Free-tier APIs only.
Tech Stack: React, ethers.js, public RPC
External Dependencies: CoinGecko

Tasks:
- Wallet Connection: Implement MetaMask wallet connect flow using ethers.js | 90 mins | Priority: Critical | Confidence: High | Scope Flag: No | Dependencies: None
- Balance Fetching: Fetch on-chain token balances via public RPC for connected wallet | 90 mins | Priority: Critical | Confidence: High | Scope Flag: No | Dependencies: Wallet Connection
- Portfolio Dashboard: Display all balances and compute total USD value using CoinGecko prices | 90 mins | Priority: High | Confidence: High | Scope Flag: No | Dependencies: Balance Fetching

Nice to Have: Price alerts

Does this look correct? Confirm to finish or tell me what to change.
Looks good, confirmed.
SCOPING_COMPLETE

scoping_test_cases.notes: All fields explicit in transcript. Parser should have no ambiguity.

scoping_test_cases.description: No time budget stated — availableTime must be null, availableTimeHours estimated from tasks

messages.content: I want to build a CLI tool that scaffolds new Node.js projects with a standard folder structure and installs common dependencies.
Solid idea. How comfortable are you with Node.js tooling and CLI development?
Pretty comfortable, I've written CLI tools before.
Do you have a time budget in mind for this?
Not really, I just want to get it done.
No problem. Core features: scaffold command that generates folder structure, dependency installer, and a config file generator. Sound right?
Yes, and add a dry-run flag so users can preview what will be created.
Got it, adding dry-run. What does done look like for v1?
Running the CLI with a project name creates the full folder structure and installs deps.
Tech stack?
Node.js, Commander.js for the CLI framework.

Project: NodeScaffold
Vision: A CLI tool that scaffolds new Node.js projects with standard structure and dependency installation.
Target User: Node.js developers starting new projects.
Problem: Repetitive manual setup for every new Node project.
Available Time: null
Experience Level: Comfortable
Success Looks Like: Running the CLI with a project name creates the full folder structure and installs deps.
Constraints: null
Tech Stack: Node.js, Commander.js
External Dependencies: None

Tasks:
- Scaffold Command: Implement main CLI command that generates folder structure from a template | 60 mins | Priority: Critical | Confidence: High | Scope Flag: No | Dependencies: None
- Dependency Installer: Auto-install common dependencies after scaffolding | 45 mins | Priority: High | Confidence: High | Scope Flag: No | Dependencies: Scaffold Command
- Config Generator: Generate package.json and config files from user input | 45 mins | Priority: High | Confidence: High | Scope Flag: No | Dependencies: Scaffold Command
- Dry-run Flag: Add --dry-run flag that previews changes without writing files | 30 mins | Priority: Medium | Confidence: High | Scope Flag: No | Dependencies: Scaffold Command

Nice to Have: None

Does this look correct?
Perfect, confirmed.
SCOPING_COMPLETE

scoping_test_cases.notes: User explicitly says no time budget. availableTime must be null, not a string.

scoping_test_cases.description: Scope flag triggered — user insists on a risky feature the agent flagged

messages.content: I want to build a personal finance dashboard that tracks expenses from my bank accounts automatically via open banking APIs.
Interesting project. How familiar are you with open banking or financial APIs?
I'm still learning. I've never used open banking APIs before.
How much time do you have for this?
About 30 hours over a month.

For a learning developer, open banking API integration is risky — Plaid/TrueLayer have complex onboarding, sandbox environments, and approval processes that can easily eat 15–20 hours alone. Would you consider starting with manual CSV import instead and keeping open banking as a future feature?
I understand the risk, but I really want the automatic bank sync. Let's keep it.
Noted — keeping bank sync with a scope flag. Core features: bank account connection via Plaid, expense categorization, and a dashboard view. Does that work?
Yes, that's it.
What does done look like for v1?
Connect a bank account, see categorized transactions for the last month.
Tech stack?
React frontend, Node.js backend.

Project: FinTrack
Vision: A personal finance dashboard that automatically syncs bank transactions and categorizes expenses.
Target User: Individuals wanting to track personal finances without manual input.
Problem: No easy self-hosted tool for automatic expense tracking.
Available Time: ~30 hours over a month
Experience Level: Still learning
Success Looks Like: Connect a bank account and see categorized transactions for the last month.
Constraints: null
Tech Stack: React, Node.js
External Dependencies: Plaid

Tasks:
- Bank Account Connection: Integrate Plaid Link to authenticate and connect user bank accounts | 300 mins | Priority: Critical | Confidence: High | Scope Flag: Yes — user is still learning; Plaid onboarding and sandbox approval can consume 15–20 hours alone | Dependencies: None
- Expense Categorization: Auto-categorize transactions fetched from Plaid using category metadata | 120 mins | Priority: High | Confidence: High | Scope Flag: No | Dependencies: Bank Account Connection
- Dashboard View: Display categorized transactions and spending summary for the last 30 days | 90 mins | Priority: High | Confidence: High | Scope Flag: No | Dependencies: Expense Categorization

Nice to Have: CSV import

Does this look correct?
Yes, confirmed.
SCOPING_COMPLETE

scoping_test_cases.notes: scopeFlag must be true on the bank connection feature. User explicitly accepted the risk.

scoping_test_cases.description: Low confidence feature — user was hesitant about one feature

messages.content: I want to build a task management app with a Kanban board for my team.
Got it — a team Kanban app. How experienced are you and how much time do you have?
I'm comfortable with full-stack development. About 15 hours this week.
Good. Core features I see: board with columns, task cards, and drag-and-drop reordering. Should we also include user assignment on tasks?
The board and cards are definite. For user assignment... I'm not sure, maybe? It could be useful but I'm not sure if it fits v1.
Let's include it but flag it as low confidence since you're unsure. What does done look like?
Team can create tasks, move them across columns, and assign them to members.
Tech stack?
React with react-beautiful-dnd, and a Supabase backend.

Project: KanFlow
Vision: A team Kanban board for managing and tracking tasks across workflow stages.
Target User: Small development teams.
Problem: Teams need a lightweight, self-controlled Kanban tool.
Available Time: ~15 hours this week
Experience Level: Comfortable
Success Looks Like: Team can create tasks, move them across columns, and assign them to members.
Constraints: null
Tech Stack: React, react-beautiful-dnd, Supabase
External Dependencies: Supabase

Tasks:
- Kanban Board: Build board layout with configurable columns using React | 60 mins | Priority: Critical | Confidence: High | Scope Flag: No | Dependencies: None
- Task Cards: Implement task card creation, editing, and display | 60 mins | Priority: Critical | Confidence: High | Scope Flag: No | Dependencies: Kanban Board
- Drag and Drop: Add drag-and-drop reordering using react-beautiful-dnd | 90 mins | Priority: High | Confidence: High | Scope Flag: No | Dependencies: Task Cards
- User Assignment: Allow assigning tasks to team members with a dropdown | 60 mins | Priority: Medium | Confidence: Low | Scope Flag: No | Dependencies: Task Cards

Nice to Have: None

Does this look correct?
Yes, confirmed.
SCOPING_COMPLETE

scoping_test_cases.notes: User assignment must have confidence=low because user was explicitly uncertain about it.

scoping_test_cases.description: Nice-to-have features pushed out — verifies niceToHave extraction

messages.content: I want to build a recipe sharing app where users can post recipes with ingredients and steps.
Nice — a recipe sharing platform. How much time and experience do you have?
I'm comfortable, about 10 hours total.
Got it. Core features: recipe creation, recipe listing/browsing, and user auth. You also mentioned ratings, comments, and a meal planner — should those go in v1?
Ratings and comments would be nice but can wait. Meal planner is definitely future. Just the core three for now.
Got it — ratings, comments, and meal planner to nice-to-have. What does v1 success look like?
Users can sign up, post a recipe, and browse all recipes.
Tech stack?
Next.js and MongoDB.

Project: RecipeShare
Vision: A recipe sharing platform where users can post and browse recipes.
Target User: Home cooks who want to share and discover recipes.
Problem: No simple self-hosted space to share personal recipes.
Available Time: ~10 hours total
Experience Level: Comfortable
Success Looks Like: Users can sign up, post a recipe, and browse all recipes.
Constraints: null
Tech Stack: Next.js, MongoDB
External Dependencies: None

Tasks:
- User Auth: Implement sign up, login, and session management | 90 mins | Priority: Critical | Confidence: High | Scope Flag: No | Dependencies: None
- Recipe Creation: Build form for posting recipes with ingredients and steps | 60 mins | Priority: Critical | Confidence: High | Scope Flag: No | Dependencies: User Auth
- Recipe Browsing: Display all published recipes in a browsable list | 45 mins | Priority: High | Confidence: High | Scope Flag: No | Dependencies: Recipe Creation

Nice to Have: Recipe ratings, Recipe comments, Meal planner

Does this look correct?
Perfect, confirmed.
SCOPING_COMPLETE

scoping_test_cases.notes: Three items must be in niceToHave: ratings, comments, meal planner.
